race.comment: Great race. PARVENU (7) was a dominant maiden winner at Cranbourne two back, just finding Pula too strong last time when getting way too far back in a small field on wet ground. Up to 1800m will suit and if she settles closer she'll be very hard to hold out. CANNAE (2) is having his fifth run of his first campaign but continues to race well and comes through clearly the strongest form lines on paper, placing in a pair of Listed race in Adelaide at his past couple. They probably haven't been the strongest stakes races but it reads well for this and he draws for every hope. BUTHELEZI (3) resumes, formerly with Dan Morton in Perth. He was last seen running fifth to a top colt in King Of Light in the G2 WA Guineas, expecting that form to hold up strongly. THERE WERE ROSES (9) won easily fresh at Pakenham and was measuring up to good fillies races in the Spring. She draws wide but can be charging late. BRAVE DANZA (1) will find this easier than the G2 Tulloch last time, consider.
